observations.reason: This is reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT and SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the Municipal Treasurer as required under Sec 20, of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol I to fairly present the receivable accounts and their contra accounts in the financial statements.  Further, require the Municipal Treasurer to regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant with the Certified List of Taxpayers at the beginning of each year and require the   Accounting Office to reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to eliminate the discrepancy between their records and to ascertain the reliability and accuracy of the reported RPT and SET Receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management is amenable on the auditor’s finding.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities. Also separate financial statements has to be prepared and presented as part of the General Fund as required under Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125.

We recommend that Management submit the Monthly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Implementation of PAPs to the Auditor as required under Sections 4.2 4.3 and 4.4 of Local Budget Circular No. 125 to facilitate the timely review and validation as well as monitoring of its implementation.

We recommend that management submit explanation of this deficiency and stop the practice of effecting payment of transactions with incomplete signatories as required under Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Henceforth, utmost diligence has to be exercised in the disbursements of public funds to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.
We recommend that management submit the supporting documents and photos showing the detailed item specification and brand name of the rapid test kits purchased to prove compliance with FDA advisory.

We recommend that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to stop the practice of awarding contracts to ineligible suppliers. Further, submit lacking documents and justification on the awarding of the contract to supplies without License to Operate from Food and Drug Administration. Henceforth, the BAC is recommended to automatically declare disqualification of any bidder who fails to submit the eligibility and technical documentary requirements in any procurement processes conducted.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books and eventually reported in the Financial Statements.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of 2020 QRF and DRRMF-Mitigation Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure the availability of this fund to support disaster risk reduction and management activities of LDRRMC.
Further, the following journal entries should be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1 – General Fund Books
Adjusting Entry No. 2 – Trust Fund Books

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconciles the cashbooks and the accounting records at least quarterly to correct reconciling items in a timely manner and present an accurate account balance in accordance with Section 181(c) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I.

We recommend that the management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms to facilitate the monitoring of the reports, issuances and balances of the said forms especially on the checks and cash tickets which were not included in the ETRACS, in accordance with Section 51 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs Volume II and to assure that collections are completely recorded.

We recommend management to exert more effort to collect these property tax delinquencies that have been long overdue in order for the municipality to augment its income that could be used for other projects that would be beneficial to the constituents of the province, municipality and the barangay.

We recommend the immediate submission of the liquidation report to the Office of the Chairman, COA, Quezon City and submit to COA the transmittal letter of the said liquidation report duly stamped/received by the said Office as proof of compliance of the required report.

We recommend that certification showing the number of workdays of actual service rendered in a month, of an official/department head claiming RATA, be submitted to serve as basis in computation of actual claims to comply with the above-mentioned regulations. The certification have to be issued by the Office of the HRMO for department heads covered under Section 7.1 of LBC No. 103, and by Sangguniang Secretary for those officials covered under Section 7.5 of the same Local Budget Circular.

We recommend that the management create additional permanent position and hire personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant in order to effectively discharge the functions of the accounting office especially in the preparation and submission of the monthly trial balances and other required reports.

We recommend that management submit justification for the procurement of air conditioning units not included in the Annual/Supplemental APP.  The Head of the BAC Secretariat is also recommended that, henceforth, only those items that have the corresponding funds/appropriations shall be consolidated into the Supplemental APP and be included in the procurement process of the LGU.

We recommend to the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in the prescribed format and submit the same to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of the semester as required under Section 12.2 of RA 9184 and its IRR to enable the GPPB to perform its duty, among others, of evaluating the effectiveness of the government procurement system and recommending improvements in its system and procedures.

We recommend that management implement the projects identified within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund considering the increasing prices of materials in order to address the needs of the community and adhere to the provision of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommend that management create a GAD Focal Point System to ascertain the alignment of GAD agenda and propose activities identified as gender issues. Also, we recommend the establishment of GAD database/sex-disaggregated data to come up with an evidence-based programs, activities/projects intended for gender causes. Moreover, maximize the utilization of GAD funds in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommend that management maximize the utilization of the GAD budget so that activities, programs and other relevant issues relevant to GAD will be addressed and prioritized rather than saving substantial portion of the budget to other expenditures defying the purpose for which the GAD budget is institutionalized.
We recommend that management maintain its Petty Cash Fund under the Imprest System in accordance with Sec. 42 of the New Government Accounting Standards for LGUs.

observations.reason: Management maintained its Petty Cash Fund under the Imprest System.
